seoTitle: How to Get Google Opinion Rewards Into PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how Google Opinion Rewards automatically pays iOS users through PayPal, and how to claim your first transfer and match your account emails.
intro: iOS users on Google Opinion Rewards receive cash deposits automatically sent to a linked PayPal account rather than Google Play credit. This guide covers matching your emails, claiming your first payout, and understanding the automatic payout cycle. It's for anyone earning survey rewards who wants their cash to land in PayPal correctly.

steps.heading: Check your rewards balance
steps.body: Open Google Opinion Rewards and confirm your balance has crossed the $2.00 minimum payout threshold.

steps.heading: Match your emails
steps.body: In PayPal's Security settings, click Emails and add your Google Rewards email as a secondary address if it doesn't match your primary one.

steps.heading: Watch for the automatic transfer
steps.body: Google automatically initiates the payout once your balance clears the threshold, no manual transfer button is needed.

steps.heading: Check your inbox for the payment alert
steps.body: Look for an email titled something like 'Google LLC has sent you a payment' and click through to accept it.

steps.heading: Verify in your Activity feed
steps.body: Refresh your PayPal Activity ledger to confirm the completed incoming transfer.

faq.question: Why don't Android users get PayPal payouts?
faq.answer: Android users typically receive Google Play Store credit instead of cash, while iOS users get direct PayPal deposits.

faq.question: What's the minimum payout amount?
faq.answer: The minimum threshold to trigger an automated transfer is $2.00.

faq.question: What happens if I don't claim my first transfer in time?
faq.answer: Initial unclaimed transfers can expire if not accepted within the mandatory 30-day window, so check your email promptly.

faq.question: Can I change which email receives my rewards?
faq.answer: You can add alternative emails to your PayPal account to clear transaction blocks caused by mismatched addresses.
